config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I think society matters a lot, but it should not automatically come before the rights of the individual, because individual rights are what protect people from abuse by the majority or by the state. In my view, a good society is built by balancing the common good with strong protections for personal freedom, dignity, and fairness. If society always takes priority, it becomes too easy to justify injustice in the name of order or progress.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan daripada hak individu. Dari perspektif saya di Amerika Serikat, sejarah kami justru menunjukkan bahwa ketika mayoritas atau negara diberi terlalu banyak kuasa atas individu, hasilnya bisa berupa diskriminasi, penindasan, dan penyalahgunaan wewenang. Hak individu memang tidak absolut, tetapi mereka harus menjadi pagar utama agar “demi kepentingan bersama” tidak dipakai sebagai alasan untuk melanggar kebebasan dan martabat orang. Masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang selalu mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang mampu menyeimbangkan keamanan publik dengan perlindungan hak dasar setiap orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Society should not take priority over the individual as a general rule, because that logic can easily be used to excuse abuse in the name of harmony or development. From my Indonesian perspective, I understand the appeal of collective interests, since we often value gotong royong and social order, but I have also seen how “for the common good” can be used to silence criticism or ignore minority rights. The state and society should protect the public interest, yes, but not by treating individual rights as disposable. A fair society is one that balances both, while keeping basic rights as a firm limit on what the majority can demand.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan secara umum atas hak individu. Dari perspektif saya di Amerika Serikat, justru pengalaman sejarah kami menunjukkan bahwa ketika negara atau mayoritas diberi terlalu banyak ruang atas nama “kepentingan bersama,” hasilnya sering diskriminasi, pembungkaman, dan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Hak individu memang bisa dibatasi dalam situasi tertentu, tetapi itu harus menjadi pengecualian yang ketat, bukan prinsip utama. Masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang mengorbankan orang per orang demi slogan kolektif, melainkan yang melindungi martabat dan kebebasan dasar sambil tetap menjaga kepentingan publik.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support the statement as a general rule, because in Indonesia I have seen how easily “the interests of society” can be used to pressure people into silence or conformity. Gotong royong is important, but it should not become an excuse to treat minority rights, free expression, or personal dignity as less important than convenience or social harmony. In my view, society is strongest when it protects individual rights first as a boundary, then pursues the common good within that boundary. If we reverse that order, it becomes too easy to justify injustice in the name of unity.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu sebagai prinsip umum, karena dari perspektif saya di Amerika Serikat, mayoritas dan negara terlalu mudah menyalahgunakan frasa “kepentingan masyarakat” untuk menekan kebebasan individu. Hak individu memang bukan tanpa batas, tetapi justru harus menjadi batas utama agar kebijakan publik tidak berubah menjadi pembenaran untuk diskriminasi atau pembungkaman. Saya juga melihat poin tentang gotong royong dan harmoni sosial, tetapi bagi saya nilai itu baru sehat kalau tidak mengorbankan martabat, kebebasan bicara, dan perlindungan minoritas. Masyarakat yang baik bukan yang selalu menang atas individu, melainkan yang mampu mengejar kepentingan bersama tanpa merampas hak dasar orang per orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
